sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27
sources.population: Adults with suspected or established hypertension.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth hypertension assessment and follow-up; partial for a general setting-agnostic follow-up workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth hypertension scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — blood-pressure assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies office, repeated home, and ambulatory blood-pressure assessment and hypertension staging.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Management — monitoring and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes monitoring phases, clinician-judged laboratory review, lifestyle domains, and monthly review context.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Referral Criteria and Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — tests required for hypertension
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists follow-up electrolytes, renal function, lipids, urine ACR, glucose or HbA1c, liver tests, blood count, and ECG according to medication and result context.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Hypertension in adults: diagnosis and management
sources.population: Adults undergoing blood-pressure measurement, hypertension assessment, or monitoring, including adults with type 2 diabetes where specified.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary care, outpatient review, home or ambulatory monitoring, and specialist referral pathways.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for blood-pressure device technique, clinic measurement, ABPM and HBPM result review, cardiovascular-risk context, monitoring, annual review, and documented high-risk features. Diagnostic conclusions, targets, treatment, medicines, and referrals are not generated or mapped. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management. The postural-hypotension section additionally supports clinician-entered orthostatic blood-pressure documentation without automatic diagnosis or management.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.8 - training, device maintenance, measurement technique, and postural assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports validated and maintained devices, standardized measurement conditions, appropriate cuff size, pulse review, and clinician-recorded postural measurements.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1-1.2.13 - clinic, ambulatory, and home blood-pressure assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting repeated clinic readings, ABPM or HBPM source and averages, result context, annual measurement in relevant diabetes care, and clinician review without automatically diagnosing hypertension.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1-1.3.3 - cardiovascular risk and target-organ assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular-risk discussion and review of urine, blood, retinal, and ECG assessments where actually performed or already ordered.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.15-1.4.24 - monitoring and annual review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinic, home, or ambulatory blood-pressure monitoring, symptoms, medication and lifestyle discussion, and an annual review without generating targets, treatment, or advice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1-1.5.3 - severe hypertension and same-day specialist review features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting measured severe blood pressure plus assessed retinal, neurological, chest-pain, heart-failure, kidney-injury, or phaeochromocytoma features as prompts, never as automatic conclusions.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.8 — postural hypotension symptoms and standing blood pressure
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting postural dizziness or falls context, lying or seated and standing blood pressure measurements, timing, symptoms, and clinician review without diagnosing orthostatic hypotension or changing treatment.
